q:
The molar conductivity of a conductivity cell filled with 10 moles of 20 mL  NaCl solution is Λm1 and that of 20 moles another identical cell heaving 80 mL NaCl solution is Λm2, The conductivities exhibited by these two cells are same.

The relationship between Λm2 and Λm1 is
question:
The molar conductivity of a conductivity cell filled with 10 moles of 20 mL  NaCl solution is Λm1 and that of 20 moles another identical cell heaving 80 mL NaCl solution is Λm2, The conductivities exhibited by these two cells are same.

The relationship between Λm2 and Λm1 is
solution:
Λm=κ×1000M

⇒Λm∝1M

Λm1Λm2=M2M1=20801020=14×21=12

⇒Λm2=2Λm1

subject: Chemistry
q:
The reaction occurs in which of the given galvanic cell?

12H2( g)+AgCl(s)⇌H+(aq)+Cl-(aq)+Ag(s)
question:
The reaction occurs in which of the given galvanic cell?

12H2( g)+AgCl(s)⇌H+(aq)+Cl-(aq)+Ag(s)
solution:
Oxidation is loss of electron and in a galvanic cell it occurs at anode. Reduction is gain of electron and in a galvanic cell it occurs at cathode.

At anode :12H2( g)⟶H+(aq)+e-

At cathode AgCl(S)+e¯⟶Ag+Cl- (aq) 

Overall reaction

12H2( g)+AgCl(s)⟶H*+Cl-+Ag(s)

Cell representation :

Anode / Anode electrolyte || Cathodic electrolyte / Cathode.

The cell representation for the above cell will be

PtH2( g)HCl soln n|AgCl(s)|Ag

subject: Chemistry
q: When an electric current is passes through acidified water, $112 \mathrm{~mL}$ of hydrogen gas at N.T.P. was collected at the cathode in $965$ seconds. The current passed, in ampere, is :
question: When an electric current is passes through acidified water, $112 \mathrm{~mL}$ of hydrogen gas at N.T.P. was collected at the cathode in $965$ seconds. The current passed, in ampere, is :
solution:
Reduction at cathode:
$2 \mathrm{e}^{-}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_2+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-}$ 
(valence factor) $\mathrm{H}_2=2$
At NTP $22400 \mathrm{~mL}$ of $\mathrm{H}_2=1$ mole of $\mathrm{H}_2 ~112 \mathrm{~mL}$ of $\mathrm{H}_2=\frac{1}{22400} \times 112=0.005 \text { mole of } \mathrm{H}_2$
Moles of $\mathrm{H}_2$ produced
$\begin{aligned}
&=\frac{\mathrm{I} \times \mathrm{t}}{96500} \times \text { mole ratio } \\
&0.005=\frac{\mathrm{I} \times 965}{96500} \times \frac{1 \text { mole of } \mathrm{H}_2}{2 \text { mole of }e^{-}} \\
&\mathrm{I}=1.0 \mathrm{~A}
\end{aligned}$

q:
The cell potential for the given cell at 298 K PtH2g,1 barH+aq‖Cu2+aqCus is 0.31 V. The pH of the acidic solution is found to be 3, whereas the concentration of Cu2+ is 10x M. The value of x is _________.

(Given: ECu2+/CuΘ=0.34 V and 2.303RTF=0.06 V)
question:
The cell potential for the given cell at 298 K PtH2g,1 barH+aq‖Cu2+aqCus is 0.31 V. The pH of the acidic solution is found to be 3, whereas the concentration of Cu2+ is 10x M. The value of x is _________.

(Given: ECu2+/CuΘ=0.34 V and 2.303RTF=0.06 V)
solution:
H2g+Cu2+aq.→2H+aq.+Cus

Applying nernst equation

Ecell=Ecell0-0.06nlogproductsReactants

Ecell=Cell potential=0.31VEcell0=0.34pH=3,Hence,H+=10-3

0.31=0.34-0.062logH+2Cu2+

Cu2+=10-7M

x=7

subject: Chemistry
q: In an electrochemical reaction of lead, at standard temperature, if  EPb2+/Pbo=m Volt  and  EPb4+/Pbo=n Volt,then the value of  E°Pb2+/Pb4+  is given by m – xn. The value of x is _______. (Nearest integer)
question: In an electrochemical reaction of lead, at standard temperature, if  EPb2+/Pbo=m Volt  and  EPb4+/Pbo=n Volt,then the value of  E°Pb2+/Pb4+  is given by m – xn. The value of x is _______. (Nearest integer)
solution:
The relation between Gibbs Free energy and emf of the cell can be related as follows,

∆G=-nFEcell

The electrode reactions can be written as follows,

Pb2++2e-→Pb       ∆G1=-2FEPb2+/Pbo

Pb4++4e-→Pb       ∆G2=-4FEPb4+/Pbo

Pb4++2e-→Pb2+       ∆G3=-2FEPb4+/Pb2+o

Now, ∆G3=∆G2-∆G1

-2FEPb4+/Pb2+o=-4FEPb4+/Pbo+2FEPb2+/Pbo

⇒4EPb+4/Pbo=2EPb+2/Pbo+2EPb+4/Pb+2o

⇒4n=2m+2EPb+4/Pb+2o⇒EPb+4/Pb+2o=2n-m⇒EPb+2/Pb+4o=m-2n

Hence, the value of x=2

subject: Chemistry
q:
What is the standard reduction potential Eo for Fe3+→Fe?

Given that:

Fe2++2e-→Fe;EFe2+/Feo=-0.47V

Fe3++e-→Fe2+;EFe3+/Fe2+o=+0.77V
question:
What is the standard reduction potential Eo for Fe3+→Fe?

Given that:

Fe2++2e-→Fe;EFe2+/Feo=-0.47V

Fe3++e-→Fe2+;EFe3+/Fe2+o=+0.77V
solution:
i Fe3+→Fe  E1o;

'n' factor=3

ΔG1∘=−3 ×F×E1o

ii Fe3++e−→Fe2+E2o; 0.77

'n' factor=1

∆G2∘=-1×F×0.77

iii Fe2++2e−→Fe E3o=-0.47 

'n' factor=2

∆G3∘=-2×F×-0.47

ΔG1=ΔG2+ΔG3

−3FE1o = −1×0.77×F+2×0.47×F

-E1o=2×0.47+-0.77F3F=0.057 V

E1o=- 0.057 V

subject: Chemistry
q:
The number of correct statements from the following is ___________

(A) Conductivity always decreases with decrease in concentration for both strong and weak electrolytes.

(B) The number of ions per unit volume that carry current in a solution increases on dilution.

(C) Molar conductivity increases with decrease in concentration.

(D) The variation in molar conductivity is different for strong and weak electrolytes.

(E) For weak electrolytes, the change in molar conductivity with dilution is due to decrease in degree of dissociation.
question:
The number of correct statements from the following is ___________

(A) Conductivity always decreases with decrease in concentration for both strong and weak electrolytes.

(B) The number of ions per unit volume that carry current in a solution increases on dilution.

(C) Molar conductivity increases with decrease in concentration.

(D) The variation in molar conductivity is different for strong and weak electrolytes.

(E) For weak electrolytes, the change in molar conductivity with dilution is due to decrease in degree of dissociation.
solution:
Conductivity always decreases with the decrease in concentration both, for weak and strong electrolytes.

This can be explained by the fact that the number of ions per unit volume that carry the current in a solution decreases on dilution.

Molar conductivity of a strong and weak electrolyte increases with dilution. On dilution as volume of solution increases. Thus, on dilution, ions get more apart and mobility of ions increases which leads to increase in molar conductivity of the solution.

Except B and E, all statements are correct.

 =2×t×60×6096500F

subject: Chemistry
q: The mass of silver (Molar mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ag</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>108</mn><msup><mi>gmol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> ) displaced by a quantity of electricity which displaces <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> at S.T.P. will be _____ g.
question: The mass of silver (Molar mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ag</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>108</mn><msup><mi>gmol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> ) displaced by a quantity of electricity which displaces <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> at S.T.P. will be _____ g.
solution:
Given Molar mass of Ag:108 gmol-1

Equal equivalents of substances deposited or liberated when some quantity of electricity passed through circuite in the electrolysis cell.

Equivalents of silver deposited= Equivalents of  O2 liberated.
Let x gm silver displaced,
(Molar volume of gas at STP =22.4L )

Number equivalents of a substance = The number of moles of substance × Valence factor of that substance
x×1108=5.622.4×4,x=108 gm.

subject: Chemistry
q:
For the redox reaction $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}(0.1 \mathrm{M}) \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}(1 \mathrm{M})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})$ taking place in a cell, $\mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}^0$ is $1.10$ volt. $\mathrm{E}^{\circ}$
for the cell will be $\left(2.303 \frac{\mathrm{RT}}{\mathrm{F}}=0.0591\right)$
question:
For the redox reaction $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}(0.1 \mathrm{M}) \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}(1 \mathrm{M})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})$ taking place in a cell, $\mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}^0$ is $1.10$ volt. $\mathrm{E}^{\circ}$
for the cell will be $\left(2.303 \frac{\mathrm{RT}}{\mathrm{F}}=0.0591\right)$
solution:
$\mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}=\mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}^0+\frac{0.059}{\mathrm{n}} \log \left[\frac{\mathrm{Cu}^{+2}}{\mathrm{Zn}^{+2}}\right]$
$=1.10+\frac{0.059}{2} \log [0.1]=1.10-0.0295=1.07 \mathrm{~V}$

subject: Chemistry
q:
An electrochemical cell, consist of the following two redox couples, $M^{x+}(aq)/M(s)$ $[E_{red}^\ominus=+0.15\text{ V}]$ and $Fe^{3+}(aq)/Fe(s)$ $[E_{red}^\ominus=-0.036\text{ V}]$. The cell EMF $(E_{cell})$ is recorded to be $0.2057$ V. If the reaction quotient of the electrochemical reaction is found to be $10^{-2}$, then the value of $x$ is ______. (Nearest integer)
[Given: M is a p-block metal and $\dfrac{2.303RT}{F}=0.059$ V]
question:
An electrochemical cell, consist of the following two redox couples, $M^{x+}(aq)/M(s)$ $[E_{red}^\ominus=+0.15\text{ V}]$ and $Fe^{3+}(aq)/Fe(s)$ $[E_{red}^\ominus=-0.036\text{ V}]$. The cell EMF $(E_{cell})$ is recorded to be $0.2057$ V. If the reaction quotient of the electrochemical reaction is found to be $10^{-2}$, then the value of $x$ is ______. (Nearest integer)
[Given: M is a p-block metal and $\dfrac{2.303RT}{F}=0.059$ V]
solution:
Given standard reduction potentials:
$E^{\ominus}_{M^{x+}/M} = +0.15$ V
$E^{\ominus}_{Fe^{3+}/Fe} = -0.036$ V

Since $E^{\ominus}_{M^{x+}/M} > E^{\ominus}_{Fe^{3+}/Fe}$, reduction occurs at the $M^{x+}/M$ electrode (cathode) and oxidation occurs at the $Fe^{3+}/Fe$ electrode (anode).

Cathode reaction: $(M^{x+} + x e^{-} \rightarrow M) \times 3$
Anode reaction: $(Fe \rightarrow Fe^{3+} + 3 e^{-}) \times x$

Overall balanced cell reaction:
$3 M^{x+} + x Fe \rightarrow 3 M + x Fe^{3+}$

The number of electrons transferred in the balanced reaction is $n = 3x$.

Standard cell potential $E^{\ominus}_{cell}$ is:
$E^{\ominus}_{cell} = E^{\ominus}_{cathode} - E^{\ominus}_{anode}$
$E^{\ominus}_{cell} = 0.15 - (-0.036) = 0.186$ V

Using the Nernst equation:
$E_{cell} = E^{\ominus}_{cell} - \dfrac{2.303RT}{nF} \log Q$

Substitute the given values ($E_{cell} = 0.2057$ V, $Q = 10^{-2}$, and $\dfrac{2.303RT}{F} = 0.059$ V):
$0.2057 = 0.186 - \dfrac{0.059}{3x} \log(10^{-2})$

$0.2057 - 0.186 = -\dfrac{0.059}{3x} \times (-2)$

$0.0197 = \dfrac{0.118}{3x}$

$3x = \dfrac{0.118}{0.0197}$

$3x \approx 6$

$x = 2$

Answer: $2$

subject: Chemistry
q:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction Zns+Sn2+aq⇌Zn2+aq+Sns is 1×1020 at 298 K. The magnitude of standard electrode potential of Sn/Sn2+ if EZn2*/Zn0=-0.76 V is _____ ×10-2 V. (Nearest integer)

Given : 2.303RTF=0.059 V
question:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction Zns+Sn2+aq⇌Zn2+aq+Sns is 1×1020 at 298 K. The magnitude of standard electrode potential of Sn/Sn2+ if EZn2*/Zn0=-0.76 V is _____ ×10-2 V. (Nearest integer)

Given : 2.303RTF=0.059 V
solution:
Zns+Sn2+aq⇌Zn2+aq+Sns

Given: 2.303RTF = 0.059 V

Ecell0 = 2.303RT2F log Kc

⇒ Ecell0 = 0.0592 log (1020)

⇒ Ecell0 = 0.0592 × 20

⇒ Ecell0 = 0.59 V

Ecell0 = ESn2+/Sn0 - EZn2+/Zn0

⇒ Ecell0 = ESn2+/Sn0 - (-0.76)

⇒ ESn2+/Sn0  = -0.76 + 059 = -0.17

⇒ ESn/Sn2+0 = 0.17 = 17 × 10-2

So, answer is 17.

subject: Chemistry
q:
For the cell $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})\left|\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) \| \mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{x}+}(\mathrm{aq})\right| \mathrm{M}(\mathrm{s}),$ different half cells and their standard electrode potentials are given below:
If $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Zn}}^{\circ}=-0.76 \mathrm{~V},$ which cathode will give a maximum value of $E_{\text {cell }}^{0}$ per electron transferred?
question:
For the cell $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})\left|\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) \| \mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{x}+}(\mathrm{aq})\right| \mathrm{M}(\mathrm{s}),$ different half cells and their standard electrode potentials are given below:
If $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Zn}}^{\circ}=-0.76 \mathrm{~V},$ which cathode will give a maximum value of $E_{\text {cell }}^{0}$ per electron transferred?
solution:
$\mathrm{Zn}+2 \mathrm{Ag}^{+} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}+\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}\left(n=2 e^{-}\right)$

$=0.80-(-0.76)=1.56 \mathrm{~V}$ for $2 e$

$\therefore \mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}^{\circ}$ for $1 e^{-}=\frac{1.56}{2}=0.78 \mathrm{~V}$

q: The hydrogen electrode is dipped in a solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. The potential of the electrode will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo></math>______<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>303</mn><mi>RT</mi></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>059</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> Round off the answer to the nearest integer.
question: The hydrogen electrode is dipped in a solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. The potential of the electrode will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo></math>______<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>303</mn><mi>RT</mi></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>059</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> Round off the answer to the nearest integer.
solution:
Given,
pH =3

⇒- log [ H+] = 3[ H+] =10-3

The half cell reaction is

2H(aq.)++2e-→H2g

We know,

the Nernst equation is
Ered=E0red+0.059 log [H+]=0+0.059  log 10−3=+ 0.059 ×(−3) =− 0.177   V

= -17.7×10-2V.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The cell potential for the following cell PtH2gH+aq||Cu2+0.01MCus is 0.576 V at 298 K. The pH of the solution is (Nearest integer)

(Given : ECu2+/Cuo=0.34 V and 2.303RTF=0.06 V)
question:
The cell potential for the following cell PtH2gH+aq||Cu2+0.01MCus is 0.576 V at 298 K. The pH of the solution is (Nearest integer)

(Given : ECu2+/Cuo=0.34 V and 2.303RTF=0.06 V)
solution:
Anode ⇒H2g⟶2H+aq+2e-

Cathode ⇒Cu2+aq+2e-⟶Cus

Overall ⇒H2q+Cu2+aq⟶2H+aq+Cus¯

Ecell°=ECu2+Cu°-EH+H2°=0.34 V

Ecell=Ecell°-0.062logH+2Cu2+

0.576=0.34+0.03-logH+2+logCu2+

0.576=0.34+0.032pH+logCu2+

0.236=0.032pH-2

7.866=2pH-2

pH=4.93≈5

subject: Chemistry
q:
Electricity is passed through an acidic solution of $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ till all the $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ was exhausted, leading to the deposition of 300 mg of Cu metal. However, a current of 600 mA was continued to pass through the same solution for another 28 minutes by keeping the total volume of the solution fixed at 200 mL. The total volume of oxygen evolved at STP during the entire process is $\_\_\_\_$ mL. (Nearest integer)
[Given: $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s}) \mathrm{E}_{\text {red }}^{\mathrm{o}}=+0.34 \mathrm{~V}$
$\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}+4 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \mathrm{E}_{\text {red }}^{\mathrm{o}}=+1.23 \mathrm{~V}$
Molar mass of $\mathrm{Cu}=63.54 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
Molar mass of $\mathrm{O}_{2}=32 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
Faraday Constant $=96500 \mathrm{C} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$
Molar volume at $\mathrm{STP}=22.4 \mathrm{~L}$]
question:
Electricity is passed through an acidic solution of $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ till all the $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ was exhausted, leading to the deposition of 300 mg of Cu metal. However, a current of 600 mA was continued to pass through the same solution for another 28 minutes by keeping the total volume of the solution fixed at 200 mL. The total volume of oxygen evolved at STP during the entire process is $\_\_\_\_$ mL. (Nearest integer)
[Given: $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s}) \mathrm{E}_{\text {red }}^{\mathrm{o}}=+0.34 \mathrm{~V}$
$\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}+4 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \mathrm{E}_{\text {red }}^{\mathrm{o}}=+1.23 \mathrm{~V}$
Molar mass of $\mathrm{Cu}=63.54 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
Molar mass of $\mathrm{O}_{2}=32 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
Faraday Constant $=96500 \mathrm{C} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$
Molar volume at $\mathrm{STP}=22.4 \mathrm{~L}$]
solution:
Eq of Cu = Eq of $O_2$
$\dfrac{300 \times 10^{-3} \times 2}{63.54} = n_{O_2} \times 4$
$2.36 \times 10^{-3} = n_{O_2}$
When current is further passed
$n_{O_2} \times 4 = \dfrac{600 \times 28 \times 60}{96500 \times 1000}$
$n_{O_2} = 2.611 \times 10^{-3}$
Total $O_2$ released
$= [10^{-3} \times (2.36 + 2.611)] \times 22400$ ml
$= 111.35$ ml

subject: Chemistry
q:
Copper reduces NO3- into NO and NO2 depending upon the concentration of HNO3 in solution. (Assuming fixed Cu2+ and PNO=PNO2), the HNO3 concentration at which the thermodynamic tendency for reduction of NO3- into NO and NO2 by copper is same is 10x M. The value of 2x is ___. (Rounded-off to the nearest integer)
[Given, ECu2+/Cuo=0.34 V,ENO3-/NOo=0.96 V,ENO3-/NO2o=0.79 V and at 298 K, RTF2.303=0.059]
question:
Copper reduces NO3- into NO and NO2 depending upon the concentration of HNO3 in solution. (Assuming fixed Cu2+ and PNO=PNO2), the HNO3 concentration at which the thermodynamic tendency for reduction of NO3- into NO and NO2 by copper is same is 10x M. The value of 2x is ___. (Rounded-off to the nearest integer)
[Given, ECu2+/Cuo=0.34 V,ENO3-/NOo=0.96 V,ENO3-/NO2o=0.79 V and at 298 K, RTF2.303=0.059]
solution:
If the partial pressure of NO and NO2 gas is taken as 1 bar, then Answer is 4, else the question is bonus.

NO3-+4H++3e-⟶NO+2H2O   ENO3-/NOo=0.96 V

NO3-+2H++e-⟶NO2+H2O   ENO3-/NO2o=0.79

Let HNO3=y⇒H+=y and NO3-=y 

for same thermodynamic tendency

ENO3-/NO=ENO3-/NO2

or, ENO3-/NOo-0.0593logPNOy×y4=ENO3-/NO2o-0.0591logPNO2y×y2

or, 0.96-0.0593logPN0y5=0.79-0.0591logPNO2y3

or, 0.17=-0.0591logPNO2y3+0.0593logPNOy5
0.17=-0.05911logPNO2y3+0.05913logPNOy5

0.17=-0.05913logPNO23y9+0.05913logPNOy5

0.17=0.05913logPNOy5-logPNO23y9

0.17=0.05913logPNOy5×y9PNO23

Assume PNO≃PNO2=1 bar

0.17×30.059=logy4=8.644

logy=8.6444

logy=2.161

y=102.1

∴2x=2×2.161=4.322

Answer (4)

subject: Chemistry
q: The anodic half-cell of lead-acid battery is recharged using electricity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.05</mo></math> Faraday. The amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PbSO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi></math> electrolyzed in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> during the process is: (Molar mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PbSO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>303</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mi>mol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
question: The anodic half-cell of lead-acid battery is recharged using electricity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.05</mo></math> Faraday. The amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PbSO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi></math> electrolyzed in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> during the process is: (Molar mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PbSO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>303</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mi>mol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
solution:
Charge of one mole of e-=1 faraday

Pb(s)+SO4-2→PbSO4+2e-

If 2F charge is passed then amount of PbSO4gm  deposited =303 g

So if 0.05 F faraday current passed then PbSO4 deposited  =3032×.05

=7.575

≈7.6 g

subject: Chemistry
q:
The standard Gibbs energy for the given cell reaction in kJ mol-1 at 298 K is:
Zns+Cu2+aq⟶Zn2+aq+Cu(s) ,
E0=2 V at 298 K
(Faraday's constant ,F=96000 C mol-1)
question:
The standard Gibbs energy for the given cell reaction in kJ mol-1 at 298 K is:
Zns+Cu2+aq⟶Zn2+aq+Cu(s) ,
E0=2 V at 298 K
(Faraday's constant ,F=96000 C mol-1)
solution:
For the reaction,
Cu2+aq+Zns⟶Zn2+aq+Cus
n=2, two electrons are exchanged
∆Go=-nFEo
=-2×96000×2=-384000
=-384 kJ

subject: Chemistry
q:
The photoelectric current from Na (work function, w0=2.3eV)  is stopped by the output voltage of the cell

Pt(s)‖H2(g,1bar)|HCl(aq·,pH=1)|AgCl(s)∣Ag(s)
the pH of aq. HCl required to stop the photoelectric current from Kw0=2.25eV, all other conditions remaining the same, is ……….×10-2 (to the nearest integer).

Given 2.303RTF=0.06V;EAgCl/Ag/Cl0=0.22V
question:
The photoelectric current from Na (work function, w0=2.3eV)  is stopped by the output voltage of the cell

Pt(s)‖H2(g,1bar)|HCl(aq·,pH=1)|AgCl(s)∣Ag(s)
the pH of aq. HCl required to stop the photoelectric current from Kw0=2.25eV, all other conditions remaining the same, is ……….×10-2 (to the nearest integer).

Given 2.303RTF=0.06V;EAgCl/Ag/Cl0=0.22V
solution:
Sodium metal :

E=E0+KEmax   ; Ecall0=0.22 V

Cell reaction

Cathode : AgCls+e- → Ags+Cl-aq

Anode : 12 H2g → H+aq+e-

Overall ; AgCls+12H2g → Ags+H+aq+Cl-aq

Ecell=Ecell0-0.061logH+ Cl-

Ecell=0.22-0.061log10-110-1=0.22+0.12=0.34 V

KEmax=Ecell=0.34 eV

So E=2.3+0.34=2.64 eV= Energy of photon incident

For potassium metal :

E=E0+KEmax

2.64=2.25+KEmax

KEmax=0.39=Ecell

Cell reaction

Cathode : AgCl(s)+e-  →  Ags+Cl-aq

Anode : 12H2g → H+aq+e-

Overall : AgCls+12H2g  →  Ags + H+aq+Cl-aq

Ecell=Ecell0-0.061log H+ Cl-

0.39=0.22-0.12 log H+

0.17=0.12×pH

pH=17/12=1.4166=1.42

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are the half - cell reactions :

Mn 2 + + 2 e - → Mn  ;  E ∘ = - 1.18 V 2 Mn 3 + + e - → Mn 2 +  ;  E ∘ = + 1.51 V

The E ∘ for 3 Mn 2 + → Mn + 2 Mn 3 + will be :
question:
Given below are the half - cell reactions :

Mn 2 + + 2 e - → Mn  ;  E ∘ = - 1.18 V 2 Mn 3 + + e - → Mn 2 +  ;  E ∘ = + 1.51 V

The E ∘ for 3 Mn 2 + → Mn + 2 Mn 3 + will be :
options: <math> <mrow> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>2.69 V</mtext> </mrow> </math> ; the reaction will not occur
<math> <mrow> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>0.33 V</mtext> </mrow> </math> ; the reaction will not occur
solution:
Δ G ∘ = - nF E ∘

Mn + 2 + 2 e - → Mn    ........... (1)

Δ G = - 2 × F × - 1.18 = + 2.36 F

2 Mn + 3 + 2 e - → 2 Mn + 2    ....... (2)

ΔG=-2×F×+1.51=-3.02 F

(1) - (2)

3 Mn + 2 → Mn + 2 Mn + 3

Δ G = + 2.36F +  3.02F = 5.38 F

Δ G = - 2 × F × E

E = 5.38F - 2 × F = - 2.69 V

We know that when Ecell < 0, cell is non-spontaneous.

subject: Chemistry
q:
1×10-5 M AgNO3 is added to 1 L of saturated solution of AgBr. The conductivity of this solution at 298 K is _______×10-8 S m-1.

[Given : Ksp(AgBr)=4.9×10-13 at 298 K

λAg+0=6×10-3Sm2 mol-1

λBr-0=8×10-3Sm2 mol-1

λNO3-0=7×10-3Sm2 mol-1
question:
1×10-5 M AgNO3 is added to 1 L of saturated solution of AgBr. The conductivity of this solution at 298 K is _______×10-8 S m-1.

[Given : Ksp(AgBr)=4.9×10-13 at 298 K

λAg+0=6×10-3Sm2 mol-1

λBr-0=8×10-3Sm2 mol-1

λNO3-0=7×10-3Sm2 mol-1
solution:
The concentrations of the ions can be calculated as follows,

Ag+=10-5M

NO3-=10-5M

Br-=KspAg+=4.9×10-8M

Λm=k1000×M=Specific conductance1000×Molarity

For Ag+

6×10-3=kAg+1000×10-5

kAg+=6×10-5Sm-1

⇒6000×10-8Sm-1

for Br-

8×10-3=kBr-1000×4.9×10-8

kBr-=39.2×10-8Sm-1

for NO3-

7×10-3=kNO3-1000×10-5

kNO3-=7×10-5Sm-1

=7000×10-8Sm-1

Conductivity of solution

⇒(6000+7000+39.2)×10-8

⇒13039.2×10-8 S m-1

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider the cell Pt(s)H2( s)(latm)H+aq,H+=1||Fe3+(aq),Fe2+(aq)∣Pt(s)

Given: EFe3+/Fe2+°=0.771 V and EH+/12H2°=0 V, T=298 K

If the potential of the cell is 0.712 V the ratio of concentration of Fe2+ to Fe3+ is
(Nearest integer)
question:
Consider the cell Pt(s)H2( s)(latm)H+aq,H+=1||Fe3+(aq),Fe2+(aq)∣Pt(s)

Given: EFe3+/Fe2+°=0.771 V and EH+/12H2°=0 V, T=298 K

If the potential of the cell is 0.712 V the ratio of concentration of Fe2+ to Fe3+ is
(Nearest integer)
solution:
Cell reaction which occurs:
12H2( g)+Fe3+ (aq.) ⟶H+(aq)+Fe2+ (aq.) 
Using Nernst' equation:

E=Eo-0.0591logFe2+Fe3+

⇒0.712=(0.771-0)-0.0591logFe2+Fe3+

⇒logFe2+Fe3+=(0.771-0712)0.059=1

⇒Fe2+Fe3+=10

subject: Chemistry
q:
For the galvanic cell,

Zn(s)+Cu2+(0.02M)→Zn2+(0.04M)+Cu(s)

Ecell =…×10-2 V (Nearest integer)

Use:E0Cu/Cu2+=-0.34 V,EZn/Zn2+=+0.76 V,2.303RTF=0.059 V
question:
For the galvanic cell,

Zn(s)+Cu2+(0.02M)→Zn2+(0.04M)+Cu(s)

Ecell =…×10-2 V (Nearest integer)

Use:E0Cu/Cu2+=-0.34 V,EZn/Zn2+=+0.76 V,2.303RTF=0.059 V
solution:
Cell reaction

Cu2+(aq)+Zn⇌Cu(s)+Zn2+(aq)

0.02M                               0.04M

Ecell0=ECu2+∣Cu0-EZn2+∣Zn0

=0.34-(-0.75)=1.1 V

Ecell=Ecell0-0.0592logZn2+Cu2+

=1.1-0.0592log0.040.02

=1.1-0.03log2=1.1-0.03×0.30

=1.0915=1.09 V=109×10-2 V

subject: Chemistry
q:
In the cell, PtsH2g, 1bar HCl aqAgClsAgsPts,the cell potential is 0.92 V when a 10-6 molar HCl solution is used. The standard electrode potential of Ag|AgCl|Cl- electrode is:

(Given, 2.303RTF=0.06 V at 298 K)
question:
In the cell, PtsH2g, 1bar HCl aqAgClsAgsPts,the cell potential is 0.92 V when a 10-6 molar HCl solution is used. The standard electrode potential of Ag|AgCl|Cl- electrode is:

(Given, 2.303RTF=0.06 V at 298 K)
solution:
A:12H2g→H+ aq.+e-; Eo=0.0 V
C :AgCl s+e-→Ag s+Cl-; Eo=xV
Ecell=Ecello-0.061log⁡H+Cl-
0.92=x-0.061log⁡10-12
0.92=x+0.72
x=0.92-0.72
=0.2V
